Title: Nt-acetylation-independent turnover of SQUALENE EPOXIDASE 1 by Arabidopsis DOA10-like E3 ligases

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential membrane sterols required for growth and development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly promotes AtSQE1 turnover in heterologous yeast assays but is not required for AtSQE1 degradation in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cycloheximide chase in yeast and Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast Scnaa20Δ complementation with AtNAA20; AtSQE1 cycloheximide-chase assays; Arabidopsis Atnaa20 background expressing AtSQE1-Myc
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNAA20 can functionally replace yeast ScNAA20 in promoting AtSQE1 turnover in yeast. 2. Loss of NATB activity stabilizes AtSQE1 in yeast. 3. In Arabidopsis, Atnaa20 does not stabilize AtSQE1, indicating NATB-independent degradation in planta.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value for sterol engineering based on this study because its effect on AtSQE1 is indirect and species-context dependent
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating NATB would likely have broad proteome-wide consequences beyond sterol metabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core metabolism-relevant gene because it was experimentally tested for causal involvement in AtSQE1 turnover; its effect was indirect and kingdom-specific

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squalene monooxygenase family; ER-associated sterol biosynthesis enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream, committed sterol-pathway step upstream of cycloartenol formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the direct precursor of downstream sterols
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential membrane sterols required for plant growth and development; sterol homeostasis defects cause developmental abnormalities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in seedlings; protein localized to ER
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cycloheximide chase; proteasome inhibitor bortezomib; LDAO treatment; terbinafine treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtSQE1 is destabilized by AtDOA10-dependent proteasomal turnover; inhibition of its catalytic activity with terbinafine causes protein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression of AtSQE1-Myc and N-terminal variants; cycloheximide-chase degradation assays in Arabidopsis and yeast; microsomal fractionation; heterologous expression in yeast; chemical perturbation with bortezomib, LDAO, and terbinafine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSQE1 is an ER-localized sterol biosynthesis enzyme targeted for degradation by Arabidopsis DOA10-like ligases. 2. In Arabidopsis, AtSQE1 turnover requires combined AtDOA10A/B function and the proteasome. 3. AtSQE1 degradation in planta is independent of its N-terminal acetylation status, because non-acetylatable MP-AtSQE1 remains unstable and Atnaa20 does not stabilize it. 4. LDAO rapidly decreases AtSQE1 abundance through a DOA10-independent mechanism, whereas terbinafine promotes AtSQE1 accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A control point for engineering sterol homeostasis, but manipulation requires caution because altered SQE1 stability perturbs sterol balance and causes developmental defects; useful in combination with other sterol-pathway regulators rather than simple overaccumulation strategies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misregulation likely disrupts sterol homeostasis; AtDOA10-defective backgrounds that accumulate AtSQE1 show seedling epinasty, indicating developmental costs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In yeast, AtSQE1 turnover is also influenced indirectly by NATA/NATB activity, but this dependence is not observed in Arabidopsis; direct ubiquitination by AtDOA10s was proposed but not biochemically confirmed

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DOA10-like RING-CH E3 ligase with TEB4/DOA10 domain and multiple transmembrane segments
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sterol biosynthesis enzyme turnover and sterol homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential membrane sterols required for growth and development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required, together with AtDOA10B, for efficient proteasomal degradation of AtSQE1 in planta; alone can complement yeast Scdoa10Δ defects
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: ER-associated ubiquitination machinery; specific complex composition not established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed, especially in roots, root meristem, lateral root primordia, vasculature, and multiple seedling/adult tissues; higher than AtDOA10B
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad expression in seedlings and adult tissues
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cycloheximide chase; hygromycin complementation in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; RNAi double-knockdown background; yeast complementation of Scdoa10Δ; cycloheximide-chase assays; transgenic promoter-GUS/YFP localization assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ERAD-type regulator that restrains abundance of the rate-limiting sterol-pathway enzyme AtSQE1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDOA10A is an ER-localized DOA10-like E3 ligase broadly expressed in Arabidopsis. 2. It functionally complements yeast DOA10 loss, unlike AtDOA10B. 3. Together with AtDOA10B, it is required for AtSQE1 turnover in Arabidopsis, linking DOA10 function to sterol biosynthesis control. 4. Global turnover of N-terminally acetylated proteins was not detectably altered by AtDOA10 depletion.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune sterol biosynthesis through post-translational control of SQE1, but pleiotropic roles in ABA/cuticle-related phenotypes and protein homeostasis caution against broad manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Atdoa10a mutants show ABA hypersensitivity and smaller rosettes; combined depletion with AtDOA10B leads to epinasty associated with altered sterol metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in pAtDOA10A::AtDOA10A-GUS/YFP; GPD promoter in yeast; 35S promoter for eYFP-AtDOA10A transient localization
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE236282; Nt-acetylome comparison associated study: PXD043217
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not established directly; participates in sterol-homeostasis control via AtSQE1 turnover
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously linked to HMGR activity and sterol-related phenotypes in dry2 suppressor context; not supported here as a major global Ac/N-recognin in Arabidopsis

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Brassicaceae-specific truncated DOA10-like RING-CH E3 ligase with TEB4/DOA10 domain and multiple transmembrane segments
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sterol biosynthesis enzyme turnover and likely ER-associated degradation functions
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential membrane sterols required for growth and development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly with AtDOA10A to AtSQE1 degradation in planta, thereby helping restrain sterol-pathway enzyme abundance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of AtSQE1 protein in planta together with AtDOA10A
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Tunicamycin-induced ER stress (reported as transcriptionally induced); protein misfolding stress discussed
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: ER-associated ubiquitination machinery; specific complex composition not established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed but much lower than AtDOA10A, especially in roots, root meristem, lateral root primordia, vasculature, and multiple tissues
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cycloheximide chase; tunicamycin discussed as inducing expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant plus RNAi depletion in Atdoa10a background; promoter-GUS expression; microsomal fractionation; yeast complementation tests; Arabidopsis CHX-chase assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important redundant component required with AtDOA10A for full AtSQE1 turnover in planta
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDOA10B is an ER-localized, broadly expressed but low-abundance DOA10 paralog specific to Brassicaceae. 2. It does not complement yeast Scdoa10Δ, unlike AtDOA10A. 3. Despite this, combined depletion of AtDOA10A and AtDOA10B stabilizes AtSQE1 in Arabidopsis, showing redundant in planta control of sterol metabolism. 4. The study suggests AtDOA10B may have specialized stress-associated ERAD roles.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be used with AtDOA10A to modulate sterol-homeostasis traits, especially in Brassicaceae, but its partial redundancy and possible stress-related ERAD functions require caution
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined depletion with AtDOA10A causes epinasty and likely sterol-homeostasis imbalance; single mutant shows no major obvious phenotype under standard conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in pAtDOA10B::AtDOA10B-GUS; GPD promoter in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE236282; Nt-acetylome comparison associated study: PXD043217
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as Brassicaceae-specific truncated paralog; not itself sufficient to complement yeast DOA10; likely has both overlapping and distinct ERAD-related roles
